rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/25/2020. No notes will be sent. The following issues must be resolved: (1) A corrected inventory and appraisal must be filed to remove the checking account under the conservator's name. It appears from the bank statement that the checking account was opened on the date letters of temporary conservatorship were issued to the petitioner was not owned by the conservatee unlike the savings account. The funds transferred from the savings accout to the checking account on the appointment date must be included in the value of the savings account. (2) Schedule I&A attached to the petition must be corrected accordingly.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND L. MAR REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondents' Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories is continued to March 25, 2020 at 2:30 p.m., under San Francisco Superior Court Local Rule 2.7B, for the parties' failure to lodge courtesy copies of moving papers and opposition papers. The parties are directed to lodge courtesy copies of the moving papers and opposition papers in Dept. 202 by March 13, 2020. The parties are encouraged to further meet and confer in advance of the March 25, 2020 hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WES LEE WINTERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/5/20. (No earlier date available.) No proof of publication has been filed yet. Item 3g(2) of the petition should not be completed when there is a will. Item 6 of the petition appears to be incorrect if the decedent was survived by nieces and nephews. The declination to act as executor does not appear to have been signed and dated. Item 8 of the petition does not clearly set forth the lineage to indicate whose children the nieces and nephews are. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.] "Next of kin" is not a sufficient description of the person's relationship to a decedent. The date of death Wayne H. Gehret (the alternate executor) was not set forth. The copy of the petition provided to the Examiner was not a true and correct copy, as pages were out of order.
